es.title: Análisis UX/UI MercadoPago
es.description: Investigación exploratoria sobre los desafíos que enfrentan comerciantes y clientes al usar Mercado Pago Point, identificando pain points y oportunidades para mejorar la experiencia de pagos digitales.
es.challenge: Identify the needs, frustrations, and behaviors of merchants and customers using Mercado Pago Point in order to understand how digital payment experiences can better support small businesses operating in dynamic economic environments.

sections.col1Content: The project focused on understanding how merchants and customers interact with Mercado Pago Point, identifying pain points, behaviors, and unmet needs to uncover opportunities for improving the payment experience.
sections.col2Content: How might digital payment experiences better support small businesses and their customers in environments where economic conditions, payment preferences, and operational needs constantly evolve?

items.title: Understand merchant pain points
items.description: Identify operational challenges when accepting digital payments.

items.title: Understand customer expectations
items.description: Explore payment preferences and adoption behaviors.

items.title: Discover usability barriers
items.description: Identify friction points across the payment journey.

items.title: Identify opportunities
items.description: Generate insights that could inform future product improvements.

items.title: Small Business Owners
items.description: Need a simple and reliable way to accept payments.

items.title: Independent Sellers
items.description: Need quick transactions while serving customers.

items.title: New Merchants
items.description: Need guidance and reassurance during the payment process.

sections.label: My Role
sections.intro: As Product Designer, I conducted an exploratory UX case study to understand how merchants and customers interact with digital payment solutions and identify opportunities to improve the overall experience. Responsibilities included:

items.title: Economic volatility
items.description: Merchants needed to update prices frequently and adapt to changing operating costs.

items.title: Payment method diversification
items.description: Businesses increasingly relied on multiple payment methods to meet customer expectations.

items.title: Digital adoption acceleration
items.description: QR payments and contactless transactions became essential for remaining competitive.

sections.label: Research Insights
items: Better support merchants operating in changing economic environments.

items.title: User Personas
items.description: Merchant and customer profiles to understand motivations and pain points.

items.title: Journey Mapping
items.description: Mapping key interactions across the payment experience.

items.title: Ecosystem Analysis
items.description: Understanding how the Point ecosystem fits within merchants' daily operations.

items.title: Opportunity Mapping
items.description: Identifying areas for future product improvements.

sections.label: Key Insights
items: Trust is essential during financial transactions.
Visibility of system status reduces anxiety.
Digital payment adoption is heavily influenced by customer behavior.

sections.label: Key Outcomes · Why This Research Matters
sections.content: This study highlighted that adopting digital payments is not only a technological challenge but also an operational and economic one. Understanding the realities of small businesses helps identify opportunities to build more accessible, trustworthy, and adaptable financial products.

sections.label: Challenge
sections.content: Transform a transactional experience into a simple, fast, and trustworthy payment journey for merchants who rely on digital payments to run their businesses.

sections.label: Constraints
sections.content: The project was developed in a highly dynamic economic environment where merchants needed to constantly adjust prices and adapt to changing operating costs. At the same time, digital payment adoption was rapidly increasing, making QR and contactless payments a necessity rather than an option. Additionally, transaction fees represented a significant consideration for small businesses, creating a tension between adopting modern payment methods and maintaining profitability.

sections.label: Key Learnings
items: Digital payment adoption is driven as much by consumer behavior as by technological innovation.
Small businesses need tools that minimize operational complexity in rapidly changing economic environments.
Great financial products are not only efficient; they must also build confidence and adapt to the realities of merchants' daily operations.
